project.name: buildwithnexus
project.description: A hilariously fast, agentic AI CLI harness written in Rust. Remote models via API key, or local models. Plans, edits files, runs commands with approval gates.

entities.name: agent
entities.description: Core agent loop implementing BUILD, PLAN, and BRAINSTORM modes. Contains the ReAct tool-calling loop, permission gates, hook integration, and context management.

entities.name: tools
entities.description: Tool definitions and dispatch. Contains 40+ tools (file I/O, search, shell, web, server management, skills, artifacts). Uses match-based dispatch.

entities.name: hooks
entities.description: Lifecycle hooks system supporting PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, and other events. Hooks can deny, allow, or inject context.

entities.name: config
entities.description: Configuration loading from env vars, ~/.buildwithnexus/, and .buildwithnexus/. Manages providers, skills, settings, and custom commands.

entities.name: provider
entities.description: LLM API client supporting Anthropic and OpenAI-compatible protocols. Handles streaming, token counting, and request construction.

entities.name: lib
entities.description: Library root and REPL. Handles CLI parsing, interactive mode, slash commands, and mode switching.

entities.name: workflow
entities.description: Background workflow system for scheduled and recurring tasks via /schedule and /loop commands.

entities.name: trace
entities.description: Execution trace ledger. Records tool calls, hooks, skills, and subagent events. Persisted as JSONL.

entities.name: session
entities.description: Session persistence for conversation continuity via /resume and --continue.

entities.name: checkpoint
entities.description: Edit checkpoints for undo. Captures file state before mutations.

entities.name: report
entities.description: Output sink with Human (colored TUI) and JSON (structured events) modes.

entities.name: knowledge
entities.description: Local knowledge base for structured storage of project entities, relationships, and judgment metadata. Supports evidence tracking and context generation.

entities.name: rules
entities.description: Rule engine for explicit engineering constraints. Evaluates rules against task context and produces violations with severity levels.

entities.name: verifier
entities.description: Verification layer that combines rule engine, static analysis, and test execution to validate agent output before presentation.

entities.name: crossterm
entities.description: Cross-platform terminal control. Used for alt-screen, raw mode, TUI rendering.

entities.name: ureq
entities.description: Blocking HTTP client with rustls. Used for LLM API calls.

entities.name: serde
entities.description: Serialization framework. Used throughout for JSON data handling.

entities.name: serde_json
entities.description: JSON serialization/deserialization. Used for API payloads, tool args, and configuration.

entities.name: No async runtime
entities.description: The harness uses synchronous I/O exclusively — no tokio, no async-std. This minimizes binary size, startup time, and complexity.

entities.name: Match-based dispatch over trait objects
entities.description: Tools are dispatched via a single match statement rather than dyn trait objects or a registry pattern. Favors simplicity and compiler optimization.

entities.name: Four dependencies maximum
entities.description: The harness keeps runtime dependencies to crossterm, ureq, serde, serde_json. No additional crates without strong justification.

architecture_decisions.title: No async runtime
architecture_decisions.context: Async runtimes (tokio, async-std) add significant binary size, startup overhead, and complexity. The harness makes sequential HTTP calls and does not benefit from concurrent I/O.
architecture_decisions.decision: Use synchronous I/O exclusively. ureq for HTTP, std::fs for file I/O, std::process::Command for shell.
architecture_decisions.consequences: Fast cold start, small binary, simple control flow. Cannot do concurrent HTTP requests (acceptable for sequential agent loop).

architecture_decisions.title: Match-based dispatch over trait objects
architecture_decisions.context: The tool dispatch could use a trait-based registry, a HashMap of fn pointers, or a simple match statement.
architecture_decisions.decision: Use a single match statement in tools::run(). Add new tools by adding match arms.
architecture_decisions.consequences: Easy to read, fast dispatch, good inlining. Adding tools requires editing tools.rs directly (acceptable given the monolithic design).

architecture_decisions.title: Four dependencies maximum
architecture_decisions.context: Each dependency increases build time, binary size, and supply chain risk.
architecture_decisions.decision: Limit runtime dependencies to crossterm, ureq, serde, serde_json. Solve everything else with std or hand-rolled code.
architecture_decisions.consequences: Small binary (~5MB), fast builds, minimal supply chain surface. May require more hand-written code for features like YAML parsing or database access.
